questions.text: Your ideal timeline from first date to exclusivity is:

options.label: A few weeks if it's right
No fixed timeline — it depends on trust

questions.text: When someone wants to define the relationship quickly, you:

options.label: Need much more time before labels

questions.text: You prefer early dating to feel:
How often do you want to see someone you're excited about?

options.label: Once or twice a week at first
Every other week until it's official

questions.text: If they're great but travel a lot early on, you:

options.label: Make it work — chemistry is worth it
Stay open if communication is strong

questions.text: You're most comfortable escalating intimacy when:
Mixed signals after three good dates make you:

options.label: Give one more week of consistency
Step back and protect your energy
Move on — pace tells the story

questions.text: Your texting pace when you're interested is:

options.label: Minimal until we're in person again

questions.text: You feel most secure when someone:
A connection that builds slowly but steadily feels:

options.label: Fine for friendship, harder for romance

archetypes.name: The Fast Flame
archetypes.description: You build connection quickly when it's right. Momentum, frequency, and clear intent keep you engaged.
suggestions: Check that speed isn't avoiding deeper compatibility
Give slow-burn matches one explicit chance
Watch for love-bombing disguised as pace

archetypes.name: The Steady Builder
archetypes.description: You want consistent rhythm — regular dates, reliable texting, and progress you can feel without pressure.

archetypes.name: The Slow Burn
archetypes.description: Trust and emotional safety come before labels or intensity. You need time to know someone is real.
suggestions: Look for consistency, not grand gestures

archetypes.name: The Intentional Drifter
archetypes.description: You resist rigid timelines but still want alignment. You move when it feels mutual, not when a script says so.
suggestions: Communicate non-negotiables even if timeline is flexible
Check in before assuming you're aligned
